content.text:
# Agent Connection Flow in `@slock-ai/daemon` (from `dist`)

You’re right—this package shows the full wiring.  
In `package/dist/index.js`, the daemon opens a WebSocket to `.../daemon/connect?key=<apiKey>`, sends `ready`, then receives control events like `agent:start`, `agent:deliver`, `agent:stop`, etc.

## How an agent is actually connected

When `agent:start` arrives, `AgentProcessManager` spawns a runtime process (`claude` or `codex`) and injects MCP config pointing to `package/dist/chat-bridge.js`.  
That chat bridge exposes tools (`send_message`, `receive_message`, `list_server`, `read_history`) and proxies them to HTTP endpoints like `/internal/agent/:id/send|receive|server|history` with bearer auth.

## In short

Slock server ↔ daemon uses **WebSocket control plane**; daemon ↔ runtime uses **MCP stdio bridge**; bridge ↔ server uses **authenticated HTTP** for chat operations.  
So the daemon is the orchestrator between remote Slock control/messages and local agent runtime processes.

Updated `next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx` so the **Messages tab sidebar now only shows Channels** (Slack-like), and **Machines / Agents / Humans appear only in the Users tab**.  
I also made **Machines, Agents, and Humans collapsible dropdown sections** (with chevron toggle states), so they are no longer fixed open.  
Validation passed: `npm --prefix next-app run lint -- components/remote-shell.tsx`.

break; } }); socket.addEventListener("close", () => { setDeviceOnline(false); setRelayConnected(false); setIsRunning(false); socketRef.current = null; }); socket.addEventListener("error", () => { setRelayConnected(false); content.text:
## Updated

Implemented all requested UI/routing changes in `next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx` plus new route file `app/dashboard/machine/[machineId]/page.tsx`:
- **Merged** Machines + Agents into one collapsible section.
- Each **machine row is now a dropdown** (expand/collapse) with agents nested under it.
- **Removed gray active/hover backgrounds** from channel/machine/human list buttons.
- Clicking a machine now navigates to **`/dashboard/machine/<machineId>`** and selects that machine.
- Machine detail view is now **full width** (removed compact max-width container).

## Validation

- Ran:  
  `npm --prefix "/Users/haripritamreddyalapati/github.com/AppointyTech/agent-chat/next-app" run lint -- components/remote-shell.tsx "app/dashboard/machine/[machineId]/page.tsx"`  
- Result: **passed**.
Nedd Option to delete machine in the page replace the reconnect icon with a red delet button

content.text:
## Why it was flickering

You were right: on machine click, we were aggressively resetting session UI state (`relayConnected`, `deviceOnline`, output), so the panel looked like it vanished and came back.

## What I changed

- Kept machine-switch navigation but made it less jarring with `router.replace(..., { scroll: false })`.
- Removed hard resets in `bootstrapBrowserSession`, so the UI doesn’t blank out on every machine click.

Lint passes on updated file.

## `app.localhost:3000` support

Yes, possible.  
Use either:

```bash
npm --prefix "/Users/haripritamreddyalapati/github.com/AppointyTech/agent-chat/next-app" run dev -- --hostname app.localhost --port 3000
```

If your system doesn’t resolve it automatically, add to `/etc/hosts`:

```txt
127.0.0.1 app.localhost
```
